title: Loop workflow to keep checking conditions
excerpt: I’m using the workflow function in Monday and it’s great, but it would be super helpful if when checking if conditions are met and they’re not then to loop back and keep checking until they are, rather than having to add…

posts.author: catscamb (Catherine Scambler)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m using the workflow function in Monday and it’s great, but it would be super helpful if when checking if conditions are met and they’re not then to loop back and keep checking until they are, rather than having to add blocks for every cycle.

For context, I have a trigger of “When item is created”, and then “If dropdown meets condition”, and if it doesn’t then to wait until the column changes and check again, but in order to keep checking I would need to add blocks of this same sequence ad infinitum.

posts.author: chris64sera (chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Catherine Scambler:

I’m using the workflow function in Monday and it’s great, but it would be super helpful if when checking if conditions are met and they’re not then to loop back and keep checking until they are, rather than having to add blocks for every cycle.
CatMeowSounds App
For context, I have a trigger of “When item is created”, and then “If dropdown meets condition”, and if it doesn’t then to wait until the column changes and check again, but in order to keep checking I would need to add blocks of this same sequence ad infinitum.

Unfortunately, as of my last update, s native workflow automation does not inherently support a “loop until condition is met” function in the way you’re describing, where it automatically cycles back. You are correct that the current approach would require adding multiple “wait until column changes” and “if dropdown meets condition” blocks.
